title: Timeline zoom on the Gantt views
what: Zoom controls on every Gantt timeline let you expand or condense how many days are visible — zoom in for a tight look at a busy week, zoom out to see the whole schedule.
where: Any Gantt timeline — an edit's Timeline tab, the project Gantt share link, or the Full Pipeline view. Controls sit at the top right of the chart.
how: Open any Gantt timeline view.
Click + to zoom in (fewer days, more detail) or − to zoom out (more days visible).
Click Fit to snap back to the full schedule.

title: Admin role preview (View as…)
what: Admins can preview the entire platform exactly as any other role sees it — Producer, Finance, Crew, Agency, or Client — without signing out. Tiles, pages, and menus all follow the previewed role.
where: Hub header, next to the Backup button: the 'View as…' dropdown. A purple banner at the top of every page shows which role you're previewing.
how: On the hub, open the 'View as…' dropdown in the top-right header.
Pick a role — the platform immediately re-renders as that role would see it.
Browse anywhere; the purple 'Previewing as …' banner stays at the top.
Click Exit Preview on the banner (or reset the dropdown) to return to your admin view.

title: Agency role
what: A new user role for agency partners. Agency accounts see the hub dashboard (Day in Review, My Tasks, Team Today), Team Management, AvocadoPost, and each project's logistics — with edit access only where they need it: the Gear and Deliverable tabs. Everything else stays view-only and finance stays hidden.
where: User Management (set a user's role to AGENCY). Agency users open projects from the FreePro tile; each project shows the Logistics dropdown (Schedule, Travel, Shot List, Additional Docs), Gear, and Deliverable.
how: Open User Management on the hub and set the member's role to AGENCY.
The agency user signs in and opens a project from the FreePro tile.
Logistics dropdown: Schedule, Travel, Shot List, and Additional Docs — view-only.
Gear and Deliverable tabs are fully editable for agency users.

title: Travel / Local, clickable everywhere
what: Mark any crew member or talent as Travel or Local and flip it with one click. Anyone set to Local has all travel detail — flights, hotel blocks, rental cars, and travel-tagged schedule items — stripped from their personal call sheet.
where: A project's Crew tab. The Travel/Local badge sits in the Travel column of both the crew positions table and the Talent list. The same switch is pinned to the top-right of the Add Position, crew Edit, Add Talent, and Edit Talent forms.
how: Open a project and go to the Crew tab.
In the Travel column, click a crew member's or talent's badge to flip it between TRAVEL and LOCAL — it saves instantly.
Or set it while adding/editing: the Travel / Local switch is at the top-right of the Add Position and Add/Edit Talent forms.
Local people automatically lose flights, hotels, rental cars, and travel-tagged events from their call sheet.

title: Travel tag on schedule events
what: Schedule events can now carry a Travel tag. Any event tagged Travel is automatically hidden from the call sheets of anyone marked Local, so local crew and talent never see departures and transfers that do not apply to them. The tag row is ordered All Crew, Video, Photo, Talent, Travel.
where: A project's Schedule tab. Add an event or edit an existing one, then use the Tags row near the bottom of the form.
how: Open a project's Schedule tab and click + Add Event, or edit an event.
In the Tags row, click Travel to tag the event as travel.
Tag order is All Crew, Video, Photo, Talent, Travel.
Save — the event now hides from every Local person's call sheet.

title: Review any call sheet before sending
what: Preview the exact call sheet each recipient will get, right from the email screen. Crew and producers open their personal filtered view, client contacts open the client call sheet, and talent open their own tagged sheet.
where: A project, then Send Call Sheet Emails. A Review button sits on every recipient row across the Producers, Crew, Client, and Talent groups.
how: Open a project and click Send Call Sheet Emails.
Find any recipient — producer, crew, client, or talent.
Click Review to open their call sheet in a preview window, with an Open in Tab link.
Confirm it looks right, then draft and send.

title: Arrival notes on locations
what: Every location can carry Arrival Notes — parking, entrance, check-in, loading dock — and they show up wherever the location appears, including the crew, producer, and client call sheets. Crew call sheets also no longer list Airport or Hotel locations, since crew get those from their own flights and hotel blocks.
where: A project's Overview tab, in the Locations section. Add a location or edit an existing one to find the Arrival Notes field.
how: Open a project and go to its Overview tab.
In Locations, click + Add (or the pencil to edit a location).
Type parking, entrance, and check-in details into Arrival Notes and save.
The note appears under the location on the Overview and on call sheets.

title: Start a new project from the hub
what: A Start New Project button on the hub opens the New Project form and drops you straight into the new project’s view — no need to detour through Finance first.
where: The hub in Project View — the green Start New Project button sits just below the Project View / Operations View toggle.
how: On the hub, make sure you're in Project View.
Click the green + Start New Project button under the view toggle.
Fill in project code, client, and name (a budget is created in RFP).
You land in the new project's Project View, ready to build it out.

title: Tag Unbridled crew on project to-dos
what: Assign any Project View to-do to an Unbridled crew member. Their First + Last initials show as a colored chip, and tagged tasks surface on that person’s hub dashboard so nothing slips.
where: Project View, Overview tab, the To-Do panel on the right — click the + chip on any task.
how: Open a project in Project View and stay on the Overview tab.
In the To-Do panel, click + Add Task (or use an existing task).
Click the dashed + chip and pick an Unbridled crew member from the list.
Their initials chip appears, and the task shows on their hub dashboard.

title: Gear Management overview & per-shoot dashboards
what: Gear Management is now a report of every production shoot, sorted by start date, with a calendar timeline up top. Each row shows the shoot code (with its suffix), the shoot title, who is responsible for gear, the dates, and the form of travel — plus a Quick View of the submitted gear request. Toggle between shoots that have a gear request and those that still need one. Click any shoot to open its own gear dashboard: a locked copy of the submitted request beside a live activity feed.
where: FreePro → Gear Management (toggle at the top of the Production Management page).
how: Open FreePro and switch to Gear Management at the top.
Use the Gear Requested / No Gear Request toggle to filter the shoots.
Scan the calendar timeline and the orange Countdown column to see what is coming up.
Click Quick View to preview a submitted request, or click a row to open that shoot’s gear dashboard (locked request + activity feed).

title: Gear Request tab
what: The gear request form is now its own tab at the top of the Gear dropdown on a shoot. Fill it out inline; once it is submitted the tab locks to a read-only copy so the request can’t be changed after the fact.
where: FreePro → open a shoot → Gear dropdown → Gear Request.
how: Open a shoot in FreePro and click the Gear dropdown.
Choose Gear Request (the first item).
Fill out the form and submit it.
After submitting, the tab shows the locked, read-only request.

title: Crews — schedules & call sheets by unit
what: Split a shoot into named crews (e.g. Recap Crew, Interview Crew). People tag into one or more crews (a cam op can be on both; untagged floats across all). Schedule events tag to a crew — untagged events apply to everyone. Each crew gets its own call-sheet link showing only their events and people, and day-level call times and logistics can carry per-crew overrides (blank = day default). One schedule, no double entry.
where: FreePro → open a shoot → Logistics → Crew (Crews row up top), Schedule (crew chips on events + Per-Crew Overrides in the day header), and Share ▾ for the per-crew links.
how: On the Crew tab, click + Add Crew and name your units (each gets a color).
In the new Crew column, check each person into their crew(s) — leave unchecked to float across all.
On the Schedule, tag events with a crew chip; untagged events show for everyone.
Need different call times or meal spots per crew? Open Per-Crew Overrides in the day header and fill only what differs.
From Share ▾, copy “Crew View — [Crew]” (or its PDF) and send each crew their own call sheet.
